model_narrative: We run a two-echelon production and distribution network over a sequence of months. Several production facilities make goods on normal and overtime shifts, ship them to distribution centers, which in turn ship to customer zones. Along the way we decide how much each facility produces on normal and overtime shifts, how much moves on each facility-to-center and each center-to-zone shipping link each month, how much each center handles and carries in storage, and how much demand to serve in each customer zone. The objective is to maximize total profit, which is revenue from sales net of transport, production, and inventory holding costs.

sets.name: p
sets.doc: the production facilities

sets.name: d
sets.doc: the distribution centers

sets.name: c
sets.doc: the customer zones, identified by number

sets.name: m
sets.doc: the months of the planning horizon in chronological order; january is the first month and each later month follows the one before it; april is the final month

sets.name: pf
sets.doc: the names of the per-facility data fields

sets.name: dcp
sets.doc: the names of the per-distribution-center data fields

sets.name: czp
sets.doc: the names of the per-customer-zone data fields

params.name: pfd
params.doc: production facility data indexed by facility and field name; pfd[p,'min-prod'] and pfd[p,'max-prod'] are the lower and upper normal-shift production limits in units, pfd[p,'over-prod'] is the overtime production limit in units

params.name: fdec
params.doc: first-echelon shipping cost in dollars per unit from a production facility to a distribution center; a value of zero means there is no shipping link between that facility and that center, so fdec[p,d] not equal to zero identifies the feasible facility-to-center shipping arcs

params.name: sdec
params.doc: second-echelon shipping cost in dollars per unit from a distribution center to a customer zone; a value of zero means there is no shipping link between that center and that zone, so sdec[d,c] not equal to zero identifies the feasible center-to-zone shipping arcs

params.name: dcd
params.doc: distribution center data indexed by center and field name; dcd[d,'max-invent'] is the maximum amount that may be handled at the center in a month in units, dcd[d,'hold-cost'] is the storage cost in dollars per unit per month

params.name: czd
params.doc: customer zone data indexed by zone and field name; czd[c,'min-demand'] and czd[c,'max-demand'] are the lower and upper bounds on the served demand in units, czd[c,'revenue'] is the revenue in dollars per unit sold into that zone

params.name: pc
params.doc: normal-shift production cost in dollars per unit at each facility in each month

params.name: pco
params.doc: overtime production cost in dollars per unit at each facility in each month

params.name: revfac
params.doc: a dimensionless revenue multiplier applied to each month, scaling the per-unit revenue earned in that month

vars.name: x
vars.doc: units shipped from a production facility to a distribution center in a month

vars.name: y
vars.doc: units shipped from a distribution center to a customer zone in a month

vars.name: pn
vars.doc: normal-shift production in units at each facility in each month

vars.name: po
vars.doc: overtime production in units at each facility in each month

vars.name: s
vars.doc: the storage level carried at a distribution center at the end of each month in units

vars.name: dm
vars.doc: the demand level served to each customer zone, in units, taken to be the same in every month

vars.name: h
vars.doc: the amount handled at a distribution center in a month in units, that is the goods available there before shipping out to customers

vars.name: profit
vars.doc: total profit in dollars

vars.name: revenue
vars.doc: total revenue in dollars over the horizon

vars.name: transport
vars.doc: total transport cost in dollars over both echelons

vars.name: production
vars.doc: total production cost in dollars over the horizon

vars.name: holding
vars.doc: total inventory holding cost in dollars over the horizon

constraints.name: m)
constraints.intent: The amount a distribution center has available to handle in a month must reflect what flows into it. For each center and each month, the handled amount equals the total units shipped into that center from the production facilities it is linked to, plus whatever was left in storage at that center at the end of the previous month. In the first month there is no previous month, so nothing carries over and the handled amount is just the incoming shipments.

constraints.name: pn[p,m]
constraints.intent: Everything a facility produces in a month has to be shipped out. For each production facility and each month, the sum of its normal-shift production and its overtime production equals the total units it ships to the distribution centers it is linked to.

constraints.name: s[d,m]
constraints.intent: What a distribution center keeps in storage at the end of a month is what is left after it ships to customers. For each center and each month, the ending storage level equals the amount handled at the center that month minus the total units shipped out to the customer zones it is linked to.

constraints.name: y[d,c,m]
constraints.intent: Each customer zone must receive exactly the demand level chosen for it. For each customer zone and each month, the total units shipped into that zone from all the distribution centers linked to it equals the served demand level of that zone.

constraints.name: revenue
constraints.intent: Total revenue is what is earned from selling to customers across the whole horizon. Set the revenue variable equal to the sum over every center-to-zone shipment in every month of the units shipped, valued at that zone's per-unit revenue and scaled by that month's revenue multiplier. Only shipments on links that actually exist contribute.

constraints.name: transport
constraints.intent: Total transport cost adds up the shipping cost on both echelons over the whole horizon. Set the transport variable equal to the sum over every month of the first-echelon cost, which charges each facility-to-center shipment at its per-unit rate, plus the second-echelon cost, which charges each center-to-zone shipment at its per-unit rate.

constraints.name: production
constraints.intent: Total production cost adds up what it costs to run normal and overtime shifts at every facility in every month. Set the production variable equal to the sum over all facilities and months of the normal-shift production valued at its per-unit normal cost plus the overtime production valued at its per-unit overtime cost.

constraints.name: holding
constraints.intent: Total holding cost adds up the cost of carrying inventory at the distribution centers across the horizon. Set the holding variable equal to the sum over all centers and months of the ending storage level at each center valued at that center's per-unit storage cost.

constraints.name: profit
constraints.intent: Profit ties the financial pieces together. Set the profit variable equal to total revenue minus total transport cost minus total production cost minus total holding cost, then add a fixed constant of ten.

constraints.name: s[d,'april']
constraints.intent: Each distribution center must finish the planning horizon with a minimum cushion of stock. For every center, the storage level carried at the end of the final month must be at least two hundred units.

constraints.name: h[d,m]
constraints.intent: No distribution center may handle more than its capacity allows. For each center and each month, the amount handled must not exceed that center's maximum handling capacity.

constraints.name: pn[p,m]
constraints.intent: Every facility has a floor on its normal-shift output. For each facility and each month, the normal-shift production must be at least that facility's minimum production level.

constraints.name: pn[p,m]
constraints.intent: Every facility has a ceiling on its normal-shift output. For each facility and each month, the normal-shift production must not exceed that facility's maximum production level.

constraints.name: po[p,m]
constraints.intent: Overtime output at a facility is capped. For each facility and each month, the overtime production must not exceed that facility's overtime production limit.

constraints.name: dm[c]
constraints.intent: The demand served in a customer zone cannot fall below its minimum. For each customer zone, the served demand level must be at least that zone's minimum demand.

constraints.name: dm[c]
constraints.intent: The demand served in a customer zone cannot exceed its maximum. For each customer zone, the served demand level must not exceed that zone's maximum demand.

query: Operations is putting a company-wide overtime budget on the table — total overtime production across every facility and every month kept to no more than 1000 units. What does the optimizer return for profit under that ceiling?
expected_pyomo: model.ot_budget = Constraint(expr=sum(model.po[p, mo] for p in model.p for mo in model.m) <= 1000)

query: There's a proposal to rebalance our market coverage so zone 2 is never served less than zone 4 — the demand we deliver to zone 2 should stay at or above what we deliver to zone 4. Plug that in and tell me where profit lands.

query: Engineering is pitching a uniform throughput limit at the warehouses — the handling moved through each distribution center in each month capped at 2800 units, to keep dock crews from getting swamped. What's the new profit if we apply that across the board?

constraints.intent: Total production cost adds up what it costs to run normal and overtime shifts at every facility in every month. Set the production variable equal to the sum over all facilities and months of the normal-shift production valued at its per-unit normal cost plus the overtime production valued at its per-unit overtime cost.

constraints.name: holding
constraints.intent: Total holding cost adds up the cost of carrying inventory at the distribution centers across the horizon. Set the holding variable equal to the sum over all centers and months of the ending storage level at each center valued at that center's per-unit storage cost.

constraints.name: profit
constraints.intent: Profit ties the financial pieces together. Set the profit variable equal to total revenue minus total transport cost minus total production cost minus total holding cost, then add a fixed constant of ten.

constraints.name: s[d,'april']
constraints.intent: Each distribution center must finish the planning horizon with a minimum cushion of stock. For every center, the storage level carried at the end of the final month must be at least two hundred units.

constraints.name: h[d,m]
constraints.intent: No distribution center may handle more than its capacity allows. For each center and each month, the amount handled must not exceed that center's maximum handling capacity.

constraints.name: pn[p,m]
constraints.intent: Every facility has a floor on its normal-shift output. For each facility and each month, the normal-shift production must be at least that facility's minimum production level.

constraints.name: pn[p,m]
constraints.intent: Every facility has a ceiling on its normal-shift output. For each facility and each month, the normal-shift production must not exceed that facility's maximum production level.

constraints.name: po[p,m]
constraints.intent: Overtime output at a facility is capped. For each facility and each month, the overtime production must not exceed that facility's overtime production limit.

constraints.name: dm[c]
constraints.intent: The demand served in a customer zone cannot fall below its minimum. For each customer zone, the served demand level must be at least that zone's minimum demand.

constraints.name: dm[c]
constraints.intent: The demand served in a customer zone cannot exceed its maximum. For each customer zone, the served demand level must not exceed that zone's maximum demand.

query: We're looking at a combined scenario for next quarter: a tighter overtime budget of 1200 units across all facilities and months, paired with a commitment to serve at least 1200 units of demand in zone 2. What does the optimizer return for profit with both of those in place together?
expected_pyomo:
model.ot_budget = Constraint(expr=sum(model.po[p, mo] for p in model.p for mo in model.m) <= 1200)
model.zone2_floor = Constraint(expr=model.dm[2] >= 1200)

model_narrative: We run a two-echelon production and distribution network over a sequence of months. Several production facilities make goods on normal and overtime shifts, ship them to distribution centers, which in turn ship to customer zones. Along the way we decide how much each facility produces on normal and overtime shifts, how much moves on each facility-to-center and each center-to-zone shipping link each month, how much each center handles and carries in storage, and how much demand to serve in each customer zone. The objective is to maximize total profit, which is revenue from sales net of transport, production, and inventory holding costs.

sets.name: p
sets.doc: the production facilities

sets.name: d
sets.doc: the distribution centers

sets.name: c
sets.doc: the customer zones, identified by number

sets.name: m
sets.doc: the months of the planning horizon in chronological order; january is the first month and each later month follows the one before it; april is the final month

sets.name: pf
sets.doc: the names of the per-facility data fields

sets.name: dcp
sets.doc: the names of the per-distribution-center data fields

sets.name: czp
sets.doc: the names of the per-customer-zone data fields

params.name: pfd
params.doc: production facility data indexed by facility and field name; pfd[p,'min-prod'] and pfd[p,'max-prod'] are the lower and upper normal-shift production limits in units, pfd[p,'over-prod'] is the overtime production limit in units

params.name: fdec
params.doc: first-echelon shipping cost in dollars per unit from a production facility to a distribution center; a value of zero means there is no shipping link between that facility and that center, so fdec[p,d] not equal to zero identifies the feasible facility-to-center shipping arcs

params.name: sdec
params.doc: second-echelon shipping cost in dollars per unit from a distribution center to a customer zone; a value of zero means there is no shipping link between that center and that zone, so sdec[d,c] not equal to zero identifies the feasible center-to-zone shipping arcs

params.name: dcd
params.doc: distribution center data indexed by center and field name; dcd[d,'max-invent'] is the maximum amount that may be handled at the center in a month in units, dcd[d,'hold-cost'] is the storage cost in dollars per unit per month

params.name: czd
params.doc: customer zone data indexed by zone and field name; czd[c,'min-demand'] and czd[c,'max-demand'] are the lower and upper bounds on the served demand in units, czd[c,'revenue'] is the revenue in dollars per unit sold into that zone

params.name: pc
params.doc: normal-shift production cost in dollars per unit at each facility in each month

params.name: pco
params.doc: overtime production cost in dollars per unit at each facility in each month

params.name: revfac
params.doc: a dimensionless revenue multiplier applied to each month, scaling the per-unit revenue earned in that month

vars.name: x
vars.doc: units shipped from a production facility to a distribution center in a month

vars.name: y
vars.doc: units shipped from a distribution center to a customer zone in a month

vars.name: pn
vars.doc: normal-shift production in units at each facility in each month

vars.name: po
vars.doc: overtime production in units at each facility in each month

vars.name: s
vars.doc: the storage level carried at a distribution center at the end of each month in units

vars.name: dm
vars.doc: the demand level served to each customer zone, in units, taken to be the same in every month

vars.name: h
vars.doc: the amount handled at a distribution center in a month in units, that is the goods available there before shipping out to customers

vars.name: profit
vars.doc: total profit in dollars

vars.name: revenue
vars.doc: total revenue in dollars over the horizon

vars.name: transport
vars.doc: total transport cost in dollars over both echelons

vars.name: production
vars.doc: total production cost in dollars over the horizon

vars.name: holding
vars.doc: total inventory holding cost in dollars over the horizon

constraints.name: m)
constraints.intent: The amount a distribution center has available to handle in a month must reflect what flows into it. For each center and each month, the handled amount equals the total units shipped into that center from the production facilities it is linked to, plus whatever was left in storage at that center at the end of the previous month. In the first month there is no previous month, so nothing carries over and the handled amount is just the incoming shipments.

constraints.name: pn[p,m]
constraints.intent: Everything a facility produces in a month has to be shipped out. For each production facility and each month, the sum of its normal-shift production and its overtime production equals the total units it ships to the distribution centers it is linked to.

constraints.name: s[d,m]
constraints.intent: What a distribution center keeps in storage at the end of a month is what is left after it ships to customers. For each center and each month, the ending storage level equals the amount handled at the center that month minus the total units shipped out to the customer zones it is linked to.

constraints.name: y[d,c,m]
constraints.intent: Each customer zone must receive exactly the demand level chosen for it. For each customer zone and each month, the total units shipped into that zone from all the distribution centers linked to it equals the served demand level of that zone.

constraints.name: revenue
constraints.intent: Total revenue is what is earned from selling to customers across the whole horizon. Set the revenue variable equal to the sum over every center-to-zone shipment in every month of the units shipped, valued at that zone's per-unit revenue and scaled by that month's revenue multiplier. Only shipments on links that actually exist contribute.

constraints.name: transport
constraints.intent: Total transport cost adds up the shipping cost on both echelons over the whole horizon. Set the transport variable equal to the sum over every month of the first-echelon cost, which charges each facility-to-center shipment at its per-unit rate, plus the second-echelon cost, which charges each center-to-zone shipment at its per-unit rate.

constraints.name: production
constraints.intent: Total production cost adds up what it costs to run normal and overtime shifts at every facility in every month. Set the production variable equal to the sum over all facilities and months of the normal-shift production valued at its per-unit normal cost plus the overtime production valued at its per-unit overtime cost.

constraints.name: holding
constraints.intent: Total holding cost adds up the cost of carrying inventory at the distribution centers across the horizon. Set the holding variable equal to the sum over all centers and months of the ending storage level at each center valued at that center's per-unit storage cost.

constraints.name: profit
constraints.intent: Profit ties the financial pieces together. Set the profit variable equal to total revenue minus total transport cost minus total production cost minus total holding cost, then add a fixed constant of ten.

constraints.name: s[d,'april']
constraints.intent: Each distribution center must finish the planning horizon with a minimum cushion of stock. For every center, the storage level carried at the end of the final month must be at least two hundred units.

constraints.name: h[d,m]
constraints.intent: No distribution center may handle more than its capacity allows. For each center and each month, the amount handled must not exceed that center's maximum handling capacity.

constraints.name: pn[p,m]
constraints.intent: Every facility has a floor on its normal-shift output. For each facility and each month, the normal-shift production must be at least that facility's minimum production level.

constraints.name: pn[p,m]
constraints.intent: Every facility has a ceiling on its normal-shift output. For each facility and each month, the normal-shift production must not exceed that facility's maximum production level.

constraints.name: po[p,m]
constraints.intent: Overtime output at a facility is capped. For each facility and each month, the overtime production must not exceed that facility's overtime production limit.

constraints.name: dm[c]
constraints.intent: The demand served in a customer zone cannot fall below its minimum. For each customer zone, the served demand level must be at least that zone's minimum demand.

constraints.name: dm[c]
constraints.intent: The demand served in a customer zone cannot exceed its maximum. For each customer zone, the served demand level must not exceed that zone's maximum demand.

query: Zones 3 and 4 sit inland and the freight to reach them is steep, yet the plan keeps pouring volume into both. Shouldn't the combined demand we serve across those two inland zones be held under 3500 units? What's the model seeing that makes it lean so hard on them?

query: Facility one is our cheapest line, yet the plan leans heavily on facility two's pricier output. Wouldn't it make sense for facility one's total normal-shift production to run at least 1.8 times facility two's normal-shift production over the planning horizon? What's pulling the plan toward the costlier facility?
expected_pyomo: model.lean_on_one = Constraint(expr=sum(model.pn['one', mo] for mo in model.m) >= 1.8 * sum(model.pn['two', mo] for mo in model.m))

params.doc: a dimensionless revenue multiplier applied to each month, scaling the per-unit revenue earned in that month

vars.name: x
vars.doc: units shipped from a production facility to a distribution center in a month

vars.name: y
vars.doc: units shipped from a distribution center to a customer zone in a month

vars.name: pn
vars.doc: normal-shift production in units at each facility in each month

vars.name: po
vars.doc: overtime production in units at each facility in each month

vars.name: s
vars.doc: the storage level carried at a distribution center at the end of each month in units

vars.name: dm
vars.doc: the demand level served to each customer zone, in units, taken to be the same in every month

vars.name: h
vars.doc: the amount handled at a distribution center in a month in units, that is the goods available there before shipping out to customers

vars.name: profit
vars.doc: total profit in dollars

vars.name: revenue
vars.doc: total revenue in dollars over the horizon

vars.name: transport
vars.doc: total transport cost in dollars over both echelons

vars.name: production
vars.doc: total production cost in dollars over the horizon

vars.name: holding
vars.doc: total inventory holding cost in dollars over the horizon

constraints.name: m)
constraints.intent: The amount a distribution center has available to handle in a month must reflect what flows into it. For each center and each month, the handled amount equals the total units shipped into that center from the production facilities it is linked to, plus whatever was left in storage at that center at the end of the previous month. In the first month there is no previous month, so nothing carries over and the handled amount is just the incoming shipments.

constraints.name: pn[p,m]
constraints.intent: Everything a facility produces in a month has to be shipped out. For each production facility and each month, the sum of its normal-shift production and its overtime production equals the total units it ships to the distribution centers it is linked to.

constraints.name: s[d,m]
constraints.intent: What a distribution center keeps in storage at the end of a month is what is left after it ships to customers. For each center and each month, the ending storage level equals the amount handled at the center that month minus the total units shipped out to the customer zones it is linked to.

constraints.name: y[d,c,m]
constraints.intent: Each customer zone must receive exactly the demand level chosen for it. For each customer zone and each month, the total units shipped into that zone from all the distribution centers linked to it equals the served demand level of that zone.

constraints.name: revenue
constraints.intent: Total revenue is what is earned from selling to customers across the whole horizon. Set the revenue variable equal to the sum over every center-to-zone shipment in every month of the units shipped, valued at that zone's per-unit revenue and scaled by that month's revenue multiplier. Only shipments on links that actually exist contribute.

constraints.name: transport
constraints.intent: Total transport cost adds up the shipping cost on both echelons over the whole horizon. Set the transport variable equal to the sum over every month of the first-echelon cost, which charges each facility-to-center shipment at its per-unit rate, plus the second-echelon cost, which charges each center-to-zone shipment at its per-unit rate.

constraints.name: production
constraints.intent: Total production cost adds up what it costs to run normal and overtime shifts at every facility in every month. Set the production variable equal to the sum over all facilities and months of the normal-shift production valued at its per-unit normal cost plus the overtime production valued at its per-unit overtime cost.

constraints.name: holding
constraints.intent: Total holding cost adds up the cost of carrying inventory at the distribution centers across the horizon. Set the holding variable equal to the sum over all centers and months of the ending storage level at each center valued at that center's per-unit storage cost.

constraints.name: profit
constraints.intent: Profit ties the financial pieces together. Set the profit variable equal to total revenue minus total transport cost minus total production cost minus total holding cost, then add a fixed constant of ten.

constraints.name: s[d,'april']
constraints.intent: Each distribution center must finish the planning horizon with a minimum cushion of stock. For every center, the storage level carried at the end of the final month must be at least two hundred units.

constraints.name: h[d,m]
constraints.intent: No distribution center may handle more than its capacity allows. For each center and each month, the amount handled must not exceed that center's maximum handling capacity.

constraints.name: pn[p,m]
constraints.intent: Every facility has a floor on its normal-shift output. For each facility and each month, the normal-shift production must be at least that facility's minimum production level.

constraints.name: pn[p,m]
constraints.intent: Every facility has a ceiling on its normal-shift output. For each facility and each month, the normal-shift production must not exceed that facility's maximum production level.

constraints.name: po[p,m]
constraints.intent: Overtime output at a facility is capped. For each facility and each month, the overtime production must not exceed that facility's overtime production limit.

constraints.name: dm[c]
constraints.intent: The demand served in a customer zone cannot fall below its minimum. For each customer zone, the served demand level must be at least that zone's minimum demand.

constraints.name: dm[c]
constraints.intent: The demand served in a customer zone cannot exceed its maximum. For each customer zone, the served demand level must not exceed that zone's maximum demand.

query: Storage runs bone-dry at most of our warehouses in most months, which leaves no cushion if a shipment slips. Wouldn't it be safer to keep at least 100 units of stock on hand at every distribution center in every month? What's the tradeoff that's pushing inventory down to zero?
